layers.name: Custody & Wallet Management
layers.description: Institutional-grade safekeeping of digital assets. The foundational layer of the stack.

layers.name: KYT / Compliance / AML
layers.description: Blockchain analytics, transaction monitoring, and regulatory compliance infrastructure.

layers.name: Trading & Execution
layers.description: Prime brokerage, OTC liquidity, and institutional execution infrastructure.

layers.name: Settlement
layers.description: The finality layer — where assets and cash irrevocably change hands. The most structurally complex layer in the stack.

layers.name: Reconciliation
layers.description: Post-trade books and records. The most underbuilt layer relative to its operational importance.

layers.name: Tokenization & Transfer Agent
layers.description: Converting traditional assets to on-chain form. The fastest-growing layer in institutional digital assets.

layers.name: Stablecoin Infrastructure
layers.description: Issuance, rails, and payment infrastructure. Where digital assets stops being a crypto story and becomes a payments story.

layers.name: Data & Reporting
layers.description: Price feeds, benchmarks, and institutional reporting. The credibility layer that enables institutional allocation.

layers.name: Technology & Node Infrastructure
layers.description: Blockchain nodes, APIs, and cross-chain connectivity. The infrastructure that everything else runs on.

companies.regulatory_status: Qualified custodian SD trust law, NY trust charter, FinCEN registered
companies.client_profile: Hedge funds, exchanges, broker-dealers, family offices
companies.key_differentiator: Oldest institutional custodian, created qualified custody category, broadest asset coverage, multi-sig pioneer
companies.risk_flags: Failed Galaxy Digital acquisition ended in litigation 2022
companies.strategic_move: Launched BitGo Prime for trading and lending

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, SOC 2 Type II certified
companies.client_profile: Banks, broker-dealers, hedge funds, exchanges, fintechs
companies.key_differentiator: MPC-based custody plus permissioned transfer network connecting institutional counterparties — network effect locks clients in and enables horizontal expansion across the stack
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into compliance connectivity, settlement adjacency, and tokenization infrastructure — most aggressive horizontal expander in the stack

companies.secondary_layers: Trading & Execution, Stablecoin Infrastructure, Data & Reporting
companies.regulatory_status: NY trust charter, qualified custodian, SEC registered investment adviser
companies.client_profile: Institutional asset managers, hedge funds, pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, RIAs
companies.key_differentiator: Only major custodian backed by a publicly traded, SEC-reporting parent — gives institutional compliance committees a level of transparency and accountability that private custodians cannot match
companies.risk_flags: Parent company regulatory exposure — SEC enforcement history and ongoing scrutiny creates reputational drag on custody product even when custody itself is sound
companies.strategic_move: Deep integration with USDC/Circle through Centre Consortium, Coinbase Prime bundles custody with execution and financing into one institutional offering

companies.regulatory_status: OCC federal bank charter (first granted to a digital asset firm, 2021)
companies.client_profile: Sovereign wealth funds, large banks, highly regulated institutions
companies.key_differentiator: OCC federal bank charter sets them apart from every other crypto-native custodian — regulated at federal banking level rather than state trust law, the gold standard for compliance-sensitive institutional clients
companies.risk_flags: OCC charter comes with ongoing examination requirements — any compliance lapse would be high visibility
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into staking, governance, and settlement services for institutional clients

companies.regulatory_status: FCA registered, MAS licensed
companies.client_profile: Hedge funds, crypto-native funds, family offices with European exposure
companies.key_differentiator: Prime brokerage adjacent — combines custody with off-exchange settlement via ClearLoop, allowing clients to trade on exchanges without moving assets off custody
companies.strategic_move: Expanding ClearLoop network to additional exchange partners, deepening prime brokerage offering

companies.regulatory_status: JFSC regulated, expanding UK and APAC licensing
companies.client_profile: Asian and European institutional money, banks with traditional finance pedigree requirements
companies.key_differentiator: Joint venture between Nomura, Ledger, and CoinShares — consortium parentage gives immediate credibility with institutional money that requires traditional financial institution backing
companies.strategic_move: Expanding client base beyond founding consortium relationships into broader institutional market

companies.regulatory_status: FINMA compliant, bank-grade infrastructure
companies.client_profile: Banks and tier-1 financial institutions building in-house digital asset custody
companies.key_differentiator: Purpose-built custody technology for banks — Credit Suisse, Citi, and others built custody infrastructure on Metaco platform before Ripple acquisition
companies.risk_flags: Ripple acquisition creates tension with bank clients given Ripple's SEC enforcement history — ongoing relationship risk
companies.strategic_move: Post-acquisition integration into Ripple's broader institutional stack alongside Hidden Road

companies.regulatory_status: FCA registered, backed by Standard Chartered and Northern Trust
companies.client_profile: Banks and regulated institutions requiring traditional banking DNA in their custodian
companies.key_differentiator: Backed by Standard Chartered and Northern Trust — purpose-built for institutions that require their custodian to have incumbent bank parentage rather than crypto-native origins
companies.strategic_move: Expanding geographic licensing footprint, deepening integration with Standard Chartered client network

companies.regulatory_status: FINMA regulated, Swiss banking law compliant
companies.client_profile: European private banks, cantonal banks, wealth managers
companies.key_differentiator: Swiss-regulated combined custody and tokenization platform — uniquely positioned for European private banks that need both asset safekeeping and tokenized product issuance in one compliant platform
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into Middle East institutional market, adding tokenization capabilities for structured products

companies.regulatory_status: SFC licensed Hong Kong, VARA licensed Dubai
companies.client_profile: APAC institutional investors, family offices, digital asset funds
companies.key_differentiator: Dominant APAC-focused institutional custodian with dual licensing in Hong Kong and Dubai — the two most important emerging institutional digital asset hubs in Asia and Middle East
companies.strategic_move: Expanding product suite into staking and DeFi access for institutional clients

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, works directly with DOJ, FBI, IRS
companies.client_profile: Law enforcement agencies, exchanges, financial institutions, government agencies
companies.key_differentiator: Created the blockchain analytics category — Reactor investigation tool is the standard used by global law enforcement including FBI and IRS, embedding them as quasi-regulatory infrastructure rather than just a vendor
companies.strategic_move: Expanding from investigation and compliance into broader market intelligence and risk scoring products

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, works with OFAC and FinCEN
companies.client_profile: Banks, broker-dealers, fintechs, crypto exchanges seeking private sector compliance
companies.key_differentiator: Fastest-growing challenger to Chainalysis — purpose-built for compliance teams at financial institutions rather than investigators, winning private sector financial services at the expense of Chainalysis's law enforcement positioning
companies.strategic_move: Deepening financial institution partnerships, expanding real-time transaction screening capabilities

companies.regulatory_status: FCA registered, GDPR compliant
companies.client_profile: European financial institutions, exchanges, fintechs requiring EU regulatory alignment
companies.key_differentiator: European alternative with GDPR-compliant data handling — preferred by firms subject to EU financial reporting requirements and firms that need non-US data residency for compliance purposes
companies.strategic_move: Expanding holistic screening product that combines blockchain analytics with traditional AML data sources

companies.regulatory_status: MAS compliant, FinCEN registered
companies.client_profile: APAC exchanges, financial institutions, crypto firms expanding in Southeast Asia
companies.key_differentiator: Strong APAC coverage with MAS regulatory alignment — particularly relevant for Singapore-based firms where MAS regulation is driving institutional compliance demand
companies.strategic_move: Expanding predictive risk scoring capabilities beyond reactive transaction monitoring

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, embedded in Mastercard global network
companies.client_profile: Payment networks, banks, financial institutions integrating crypto into payment rails
companies.key_differentiator: Mastercard acquisition signals the end game — embedding blockchain analytics directly into global payment infrastructure rather than selling it as a standalone compliance product
companies.risk_flags: Mastercard ownership creates potential conflict for crypto-native clients competing with Mastercard's payment network
companies.strategic_move: Integration into Mastercard's crypto and stablecoin payment monitoring infrastructure

companies.regulatory_status: Works with CFTC and SEC on market surveillance standards
companies.client_profile: Crypto exchanges, institutional trading desks, regulated venues seeking FINRA-equivalent surveillance
companies.key_differentiator: Market surveillance and manipulation detection rather than pure KYT — the NICE Actimize of crypto, positioned for exchanges seeking regulatory approval and institutional desks facing trading compliance requirements
companies.strategic_move: Expanding as crypto exchanges pursue SEC and CFTC registration requiring formal market surveillance infrastructure

companies.regulatory_status: FATF Travel Rule compliant across multiple jurisdictions
companies.client_profile: Exchanges, VASPs, financial institutions with cross-border crypto transfer obligations
companies.key_differentiator: Purpose-built Travel Rule compliance — built the inter-VASP messaging layer that makes FATF Travel Rule compliance operationally workable, solving one of the most painful cross-border compliance requirements in institutional digital assets
companies.strategic_move: Expanding network of connected VASPs to increase Travel Rule message coverage globally

companies.regulatory_status: CFTC registered swap dealer, FinCEN registered
companies.client_profile: Hedge funds, asset managers, family offices, corporate treasuries
companies.key_differentiator: Most institutional crypto prime broker — provides credit, execution, and clearing to sophisticated institutional clients with the deepest liquidity and tightest spreads in the market
companies.strategic_move: Expanding prime brokerage product suite including securities lending and portfolio margining across digital assets

companies.regulatory_status: FINRA registered broker-dealer, NFA registered
companies.client_profile: Institutional hedge funds, banks, asset managers from traditional finance background
companies.key_differentiator: Built by traditional prime brokerage veterans specifically to bridge institutional TradFi and crypto — FINRA broker-dealer registration gives it regulatory standing that pure crypto-native prime brokers lack
companies.risk_flags: Ripple acquisition in 2025 creates uncertainty about strategic direction and potential client conflicts
companies.strategic_move: Post-acquisition integration with Ripple's Metaco custody and XRP payment infrastructure

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, operates under DRW regulatory framework
companies.client_profile: Institutional investors, hedge funds, corporations seeking OTC liquidity with TradFi counterparty credibility
companies.key_differentiator: DRW parentage is the entire value proposition — one of the most respected proprietary trading firms in traditional markets backing a crypto OTC desk gives credibility that standalone crypto firms cannot match
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into structured products and institutional derivatives alongside spot OTC trading

companies.regulatory_status: SEC reporting, FINRA registered, NFA registered
companies.client_profile: Institutional investors, asset managers, corporations, family offices
companies.key_differentiator: Most comprehensive institutional digital asset firm — simultaneously an asset manager, investment bank, trading desk, and mining operation, giving clients a single counterparty for multiple institutional digital asset needs
companies.risk_flags: Business complexity and multiple revenue streams create opacity — trading desk conflicts with asset management relationships
companies.strategic_move: Expanding lending and structured products, Bitcoin ETF products through Galaxy Asset Management

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 certified, works with regulated institutional clients
companies.client_profile: Banks, broker-dealers, hedge funds, market makers building institutional trading infrastructure
companies.key_differentiator: Trading technology infrastructure rather than a liquidity provider — the Fidessa or Bloomberg TOMS of crypto, providing order management, analytics, and multi-venue connectivity without taking the other side of trades
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into DeFi connectivity and portfolio management tools for institutional clients

companies.regulatory_status: FCA regulated, MAS licensed
companies.client_profile: Banks, asset managers, fintech platforms needing reliable crypto liquidity
companies.key_differentiator: SBI Group acquisition provides Japanese institutional backing and APAC distribution — crypto OTC market maker with traditional financial institution parentage, similar to Cumberland's DRW relationship
companies.strategic_move: Expanding APAC distribution through SBI network, adding options and structured product capabilities

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, FCA registered, MAS licensed — complex multi-jurisdictional regulatory history
companies.client_profile: Institutional and retail traders, increasingly institutional-focused through Kraken Institutional
companies.key_differentiator: One of the oldest and most liquid crypto exchanges globally — NinjaTrader acquisition signals expansion into traditional derivatives infrastructure alongside CF Benchmarks ownership creating vertical integration of exchange plus benchmark
companies.risk_flags: Ongoing US regulatory scrutiny, SEC settlement over staking program 2023, historically confrontational posture with US regulators
companies.strategic_move: NinjaTrader acquisition 2025, Kraken Institutional expansion, leveraging CF Benchmarks for institutional derivatives positioning

companies.regulatory_status: Works with CFTC, FCA regulated clients
companies.client_profile: Crypto exchanges, institutional trading desks, regulated venues
companies.key_differentiator: Trade surveillance technology for crypto venues and institutional desks — as crypto exchanges pursue regulatory approval, formal surveillance infrastructure becomes mandatory and Eventus is positioned to be the compliance standard
companies.strategic_move: Expanding as regulatory requirements for crypto venue surveillance increase under new US crypto legislation

companies.regulatory_status: SEC regulated, CFTC regulated, Federal Reserve oversight — systemically important financial market utility
companies.client_profile: All major US broker-dealers, banks, asset managers — effectively mandatory infrastructure
companies.key_differentiator: Incumbent settlement utility processing trillions daily — digital assets initiatives represent the establishment's attempt to extend utility status into digital assets before crypto-native players make them irrelevant
companies.risk_flags: Member-owned governance means innovation moves at the speed of consensus among competing institutions
companies.strategic_move: Project Ion for accelerated settlement, digital securities sandbox, exploring tokenized settlement finality

companies.public_private: Consortium (Goldman Sachs, BNY Mellon, Deloitte, Microsoft, others)
companies.regulatory_status: Built on Daml smart contract language, participants are individually regulated
companies.client_profile: Tier-1 banks, asset managers, financial infrastructure firms building shared settlement infrastructure
companies.key_differentiator: Privacy-enabled blockchain built by the most credible institutional consortium in digital assets — privacy-preserving architecture solves the core reason institutions won't settle on public blockchains
companies.risk_flags: Consortium governance creates coordination risk — competing member interests slow deployment
companies.strategic_move: Live pilots in repo and securities settlement, expanding membership and transaction volume

companies.regulatory_status: Bank of England oversight, regulated as payment system
companies.client_profile: Central banks, tier-1 banks, financial institutions requiring central bank money finality
companies.key_differentiator: Tokenized central bank money — Fnality tokens represent claims on reserves held at central banks, giving settlement the same legal finality as Fedwire or CHAPS, which no other crypto settlement mechanism can claim
companies.risk_flags: Regulatory approval timeline uncertainty — Bank of England oversight means deployment pace is externally controlled
companies.strategic_move: UK regulatory approval progressing, expanding to additional central bank currency pairs beyond GBP

companies.regulatory_status: Deutsche Bundesbank oversight, works within ECB regulatory framework
companies.client_profile: Banks, broker-dealers managing collateral and securities financing
companies.key_differentiator: DLT-based collateral mobility — allows high-quality liquid assets to move between counterparties in near real-time to meet margin requirements, solving one of the most capital-intensive operational problems in institutional finance
companies.strategic_move: Expanding asset class coverage beyond government bonds into broader HQLA eligible securities

companies.regulatory_status: SEC registered, OCC engagement, state lending licenses
companies.client_profile: Mortgage originators, asset managers, financial institutions processing loan and credit assets
companies.key_differentiator: Only player that built and deployed a working proprietary blockchain for real financial asset settlement at scale — billions in loan origination processed on Provenance, not a pilot or sandbox but live production infrastructure
companies.risk_flags: Proprietary blockchain creates ecosystem dependency — clients building on Provenance are locked into Figure's infrastructure choices
companies.strategic_move: Expanding Provenance blockchain use cases beyond lending into broader securities settlement and tokenized asset registry

assets.name: Figure USD Yield
assets.note: First stablecoin registered with the SEC as a paying security — holders receive yield and have registered security protections that USDC and USDT holders do not. Figure the company is privately held — YLDS is not equity.

issued_assets.tokenization_note: Also operates Provenance Blockchain as proprietary settlement infrastructure. Blockchain is secondary to the core lending business.

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 1 and SOC 2 certified, works with Big 4 auditors
companies.client_profile: Fund administrators, institutional asset managers, hedge funds holding digital assets
companies.key_differentiator: Most institutionally credible pure-play crypto recon firm — Big 4 auditor relationships and fund administrator client base give them the compliance credibility that crypto-native recon startups lack
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into broader fund administration services beyond recon, deepening Big 4 auditor partnerships

companies.regulatory_status: Works with IRS-compliant reporting standards
companies.client_profile: Accounting firms, tax professionals, HNW individuals and institutions with crypto tax reporting obligations
companies.key_differentiator: Tax reporting and accounting focus rather than pure operational recon — primary clients are the accounting firms and tax professionals handling crypto for institutions, not the institutions themselves
companies.strategic_move: Expanding enterprise product for institutional crypto tax reporting as IRS reporting requirements tighten

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 in progress
companies.client_profile: Crypto-native companies, exchanges, protocols, crypto treasury teams
companies.key_differentiator: Purpose-built for crypto-native treasury and finance teams rather than traditional institutions — cleaner and faster than legacy alternatives but institutional credibility with traditional finance compliance teams still developing
companies.strategic_move: Expanding from crypto-native clients toward traditional institutions holding digital assets on their balance sheet

companies.regulatory_status: Works with SEC and CFTC regulated entities, backed by regulated financial institutions
companies.client_profile: Tier-1 banks, broker-dealers, financial infrastructure firms
companies.key_differentiator: DLT-based post-trade infrastructure backed by Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, and others — builds the rails that reconciliation runs on rather than selling a recon product directly, making them infrastructure for the infrastructure
companies.strategic_move: Expanding post-trade infrastructure use cases including derivatives processing and collateral management

companies.regulatory_status: SEC registered investment adviser, multiple state and international licenses
companies.client_profile: Hedge funds, asset managers, fund administrators using Advent Geneva for portfolio management and recon
companies.key_differentiator: Dominant legacy fund administration and recon platform — digital asset support bolted on rather than native, creating operational gaps that crypto-native vendors exploit but distribution through existing client relationships is unmatched
companies.risk_flags: Legacy architecture not designed for 24/7 crypto markets — operational gaps in digital asset recon create client vulnerability to crypto-native competitors
companies.strategic_move: Adding digital asset modules to Advent Geneva, acquiring or partnering with crypto-native recon vendors

companies.regulatory_status: SEC registered, FINRA regulated, multiple international licenses
companies.client_profile: Broker-dealers, asset managers, banks — existing client relationships cover most of Wall Street
companies.key_differentiator: Legacy incumbent with the broadest existing client relationships in financial services — DLT Repo platform processes billions in repo transactions daily making it one of the highest-volume deployed blockchain use cases in traditional finance
companies.risk_flags: Digital assets capabilities immature relative to the complexity of the problem — existing client relationships create adoption pathway but technology lags crypto-native competitors
companies.strategic_move: DLT Repo expansion, proxy voting on DLT, digital securities post-trade processing

issued_assets.tokenization_note: Operates DLT Repo platform processing billions in repo transactions daily — one of the highest-volume deployed blockchain use cases in traditional finance. No token issued.

companies.regulatory_status: Works with SEC registered fund administrators
companies.client_profile: Fund administrators specifically rather than the funds themselves
companies.key_differentiator: Focused specifically on fund administrators rather than funds directly — smart wedge into the workflow where reconciliation work actually happens daily, underserved by both legacy systems and crypto-native startups
companies.strategic_move: Deepening fund administrator client base, expanding asset class coverage

companies.regulatory_status: SEC registered transfer agent, SEC registered broker-dealer, FINRA member
companies.client_profile: Asset managers, PE firms, alternative investment managers seeking to tokenize fund interests
companies.key_differentiator: Dominant US tokenized securities platform — SEC-registered transfer agent status and BlackRock BUIDL relationship established them as the de facto standard for institutional tokenized fund issuance in the United States
companies.strategic_move: Expanding tokenization platform to additional asset managers following BlackRock relationship, building secondary market liquidity infrastructure for tokenized securities

companies.regulatory_status: Works with SEC-registered entities for underlying asset management
companies.client_profile: Institutions and protocols seeking yield-bearing tokenized Treasury alternatives to zero-yield stablecoins
companies.key_differentiator: Creating a new product category — tokenized US Treasuries and money market funds that function as yield-bearing stablecoins, blurring the line between tokenization and stablecoin infrastructure in ways that could disrupt both categories
companies.strategic_move: Expanding OUSG and USDY to additional blockchain networks, deepening institutional distribution partnerships

assets.name: Ondo Short-Term US Government Bond Fund
assets.note: Tokenized exposure to US Treasuries. A financial product — not equity in Ondo Finance.

assets.name: Ondo US Dollar Yield
assets.note: Yield-bearing dollar instrument backed by Treasuries. Competes with zero-yield stablecoins as institutional working capital.

companies.regulatory_status: SEC registered investment adviser, multiple global licenses
companies.client_profile: Institutional and retail investors in Franklin Templeton fund products
companies.key_differentiator: Most significant signal in the tokenization layer — a $1.5 trillion traditional asset manager chose to build and operate blockchain infrastructure directly rather than use a vendor, signaling where the largest asset managers think this is going
companies.risk_flags: Traditional asset manager operating blockchain infrastructure creates regulatory complexity and operational risk outside their core competency
companies.strategic_move: Expanding BENJI token to additional blockchain networks, exploring broader tokenized fund product suite

assets.name: Franklin OnChain US Government Money Fund
assets.note: Tokenized money market fund running natively on Stellar and Polygon. A fund product — not a representation of Franklin Templeton equity. BEN (NYSE) is the equity instrument.

issued_assets.tokenization_note: Franklin Templeton built and operates blockchain infrastructure directly rather than using a vendor — significant strategic signal from a $1.5T asset manager.

companies.regulatory_status: MiFID II compliant, EU DLT Pilot Regime participant
companies.client_profile: European asset managers, private banks, issuers of tokenized securities under EU regulatory framework
companies.key_differentiator: European tokenization platform acquired by Euronext — stock exchange ownership provides unmatched institutional distribution and regulatory credibility for tokenized securities issuance under EU frameworks
companies.strategic_move: Integration with Euronext trading and post-trade infrastructure, expanding EU tokenized securities issuance volumes

companies.regulatory_status: Created ERC-1400 security token standard
companies.client_profile: Early security token issuers — limited current institutional relevance
companies.key_differentiator: Early pioneer that created the ERC-1400 security token standard — technology contributions were real but commercial momentum stalled as better-resourced and more regulated competitors entered the market
companies.risk_flags: Lost market position to Securitize and other better-resourced competitors — limited current commercial relevance
companies.strategic_move: Pivoting toward Polymesh, a purpose-built blockchain for regulated securities

companies.regulatory_status: NY BitLicense, FinCEN registered, state money transmitter licenses, EU MiCA compliant
companies.client_profile: Institutional investors, exchanges, fintechs, payment companies, DeFi protocols
companies.key_differentiator: Most important regulated stablecoin issuer — USDC with full reserves, monthly Deloitte attestations, and Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol across dozens of blockchains positions Circle as the stablecoin of institutional and regulated finance
companies.risk_flags: Exposure to banking system — Silicon Valley Bank collapse temporarily affected USDC peg in 2023, revealing reserve management risk
companies.strategic_move: IPO filing positions Circle for public market capital raise, CCTP expansion enabling seamless USDC movement across blockchains, institutional API product expansion

assets.name: USD Coin
assets.note: Fully reserved, monthly Deloitte attestations. Holding USDC confers no ownership in Circle.

companies.regulatory_status: Limited US regulatory standing — offshore domicile, historically opaque reserve reporting
companies.client_profile: Crypto-native traders, emerging market users, global informal dollar demand
companies.key_differentiator: Dominant by volume despite regulatory opacity — USDT's staying power reflects genuine utility in markets where dollar access is restricted and crypto-native trading where liquidity depth matters more than regulatory compliance
companies.risk_flags: Historically opaque reserve composition, offshore domicile creates US regulatory exposure risk, no full independent audit completed
companies.strategic_move: Expanding into non-dollar stablecoins, commodity-backed tokens, and communications infrastructure through Tether investments

assets.name: Tether
assets.note: Dominant by volume globally. Offshore issuer — holding USDT confers no ownership in Tether and no US regulatory protections.

companies.regulatory_status: NY trust company charter, OCC engagement, FinCEN registered
companies.client_profile: Financial institutions seeking to issue their own regulated stablecoins, enterprise clients
companies.key_differentiator: Most regulatory-forward stablecoin infrastructure provider — NY trust company charter and white-label stablecoin issuance infrastructure allows financial institutions to issue their own branded stablecoins on Paxos rails
companies.risk_flags: NYDFS ordered halt to BUSD issuance for Binance in 2023 — demonstrated regulatory risk of issuing stablecoins primarily associated with a single exchange counterparty
companies.strategic_move: Refocusing on USDP and enterprise white-label stablecoin issuance for financial institutions post-BUSD shutdown

assets.name: Pax Dollar
assets.note: Issued under NY trust company charter. Previously issued BUSD for Binance until NYDFS halt in 2023.

companies.regulatory_status: Issued by Paxos under NY trust company charter, PayPal state money transmitter licenses
companies.client_profile: PayPal merchant network, PayPal users, platforms integrating PayPal payment rails
companies.key_differentiator: Most significant signal that mainstream payment companies treat stablecoin issuance as a core payments capability — when PayPal issues a stablecoin every other payment company's board asks why they haven't
companies.risk_flags: Limited adoption beyond PayPal ecosystem — PYUSD volume small relative to USDC and USDT, dependent on PayPal merchant and user adoption
companies.strategic_move: Expanding PYUSD to Solana blockchain, incentivizing merchant adoption through PayPal checkout integration

assets.name: PayPal USD
assets.note: Issued by Paxos under PayPal brand. Holding PYUSD confers no ownership in PayPal — separate from PYPL equity.

companies.regulatory_status: FinCEN registered, state money transmitter licenses via Stripe
companies.client_profile: Businesses accepting, holding, and sending stablecoins across multiple blockchains
companies.key_differentiator: Most strategically important acquisition in digital assets infrastructure — Stripe paying $1.1B for Bridge signals that the next generation of global payments runs on stablecoin rails, making this a payments infrastructure story not a crypto story
companies.strategic_move: Integration into Stripe payment infrastructure, enabling stablecoin acceptance for Stripe's millions of merchant clients globally

companies.regulatory_status: FCA registered, multiple emerging market licenses
companies.client_profile: Businesses making cross-border payments in corridors where traditional banking is slow and expensive
companies.key_differentiator: Stablecoin payment infrastructure for cross-border business payments — growing fastest in corridors where correspondent banking is most broken, particularly LATAM, Africa, and Southeast Asia
companies.strategic_move: Expanding geographic licensing footprint, deepening LATAM and Africa payment corridor coverage

companies.regulatory_status: Swiss FINMA registered, FinCEN registered
companies.client_profile: Payment service providers, money transfer operators with cross-border pre-funding requirements
companies.key_differentiator: Solves the pre-funding problem in cross-border payments using USDC as working capital — eliminates the need for payment companies to tie up capital in nostro accounts by providing real-time stablecoin liquidity for cross-border settlement
companies.strategic_move: Expanding licensed corridors and PSP network, deepening USDC liquidity facilities

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 certified, data methodology independently reviewed
companies.client_profile: Institutional asset managers, index providers, exchanges, risk managers, auditors
companies.key_differentiator: Most institutionally credible pure-play crypto data provider — transparent reproducible methodology for filtering fake volume and wash trading is as important as the data itself for institutions defending data sources to auditors
companies.strategic_move: Expanding CMBI index products, deepening institutional data distribution partnerships

companies.regulatory_status: GDPR compliant, AMF engaged
companies.client_profile: Quant funds, market makers, European institutional investors requiring granular order book data
companies.key_differentiator: European alternative with superior market microstructure data — order book depth and historical tick data coverage makes Kaiko the preferred choice for quantitative strategies requiring granular exchange data rather than just price feeds
companies.strategic_move: Expanding derivatives and DeFi data coverage, deepening European institutional distribution

companies.regulatory_status: FCA regulated benchmark administrator, CME Group designated contract market benchmark
companies.client_profile: CME futures participants, institutional derivatives traders, ETF issuers requiring regulated crypto benchmarks
companies.key_differentiator: Most systemically important data company in the stack — provides official settlement prices for CME Bitcoin and Ethereum futures, making them critical infrastructure for the entire institutional derivatives market
companies.risk_flags: Kraken ownership creates conflict of interest — exchange now owns the benchmark that its own products reference, worth monitoring for regulatory scrutiny
companies.strategic_move: Expanding benchmark suite to additional crypto assets, providing reference rates for new institutional derivative products

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 certified
companies.client_profile: Institutional traders, risk managers, DeFi participants, derivatives desks
companies.key_differentiator: Strongest DeFi and derivatives analytics coverage — fills the gap between traditional crypto market data and the institutional need to monitor and analyze decentralized protocol activity and on-chain derivatives
companies.strategic_move: Expanding institutional DeFi analytics as institutional interest in on-chain protocols grows

companies.regulatory_status: Works toward standardized crypto disclosure frameworks
companies.client_profile: Institutional investors researching protocols, family offices, VC funds evaluating digital asset investments
companies.key_differentiator: Research and intelligence rather than raw data feeds — built around standardized disclosure for crypto assets without SEC reporting requirements, providing the qualitative due diligence layer that price feeds alone cannot supply
companies.strategic_move: Expanding institutional research products, building toward enterprise intelligence platform for digital asset allocation

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 Type II certified, works with regulated financial institutions
companies.client_profile: Banks, custodians, exchanges, institutional validators requiring enterprise-grade node infrastructure
companies.key_differentiator: Most institutionally focused node infrastructure provider — enterprise reliability, compliance posture, and support for 70+ blockchain protocols positions them as the AWS of blockchain infrastructure for regulated financial institutions
companies.strategic_move: Expanding staking infrastructure and validator services for institutional clients, adding institutional MPC wallet infrastructure

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 certified
companies.client_profile: Fintechs, crypto-native companies, developers building blockchain applications
companies.key_differentiator: Dominant developer platform for blockchain application development — powers an enormous percentage of Ethereum applications through superior developer tooling, APIs, and reliability, though client profile skews to developers rather than regulated institutions
companies.strategic_move: Expanding multi-chain coverage and enterprise features, building toward institutional client segment

companies.regulatory_status: Working with EU MiCA compliance requirements
companies.client_profile: Ethereum application developers, fintechs, companies building on Ethereum ecosystem
companies.key_differentiator: Ethereum ecosystem infrastructure — MetaMask, Infura, and Linea Layer 2 under one roof makes Consensys the most embedded company in Ethereum but financial difficulties and restructuring create ecosystem dependency risk
companies.risk_flags: Consensys financial difficulties and restructuring in 2024 create risk for clients dependent on Infura — when core Ethereum infrastructure has balance sheet problems the ecosystem notices
companies.strategic_move: Restructuring and refocusing on core infrastructure products, MetaMask institutional expansion

companies.regulatory_status: SOC 2 certified
companies.client_profile: Developers, fintechs, mid-market companies needing multi-chain node access
companies.key_differentiator: Fastest-growing multi-chain node provider — broader protocol coverage than Alchemy or Infura with competitive performance, winning clients in the developer and mid-market segment on multi-chain flexibility
companies.strategic_move: Expanding enterprise features and institutional client capabilities, broadening blockchain protocol support

companies.regulatory_status: Decentralized network — individual node operators subject to local regulations
companies.client_profile: DeFi protocols, tokenization platforms, any on-chain financial product requiring real-world data
companies.key_differentiator: Oracle network that connects blockchains to real-world data — without reliable oracles smart contracts cannot access external price feeds, making Chainlink critical infrastructure for tokenized assets, DeFi, and any on-chain financial product referencing real-world values
companies.risk_flags: Decentralized governance creates protocol upgrade complexity — no single entity controls network evolution
companies.strategic_move: CCIP cross-chain interoperability protocol expanding, deepening institutional partnerships for tokenized asset price feeds

assets.name: Chainlink
assets.note: LINK is the economic mechanism of the Chainlink network — node operators are paid in LINK and stake it as security collateral. Unlike other tokens here, LINK is inseparable from the network's operation. Not a stablecoin or financial product.

companies.regulatory_status: Decentralized network architecture
companies.client_profile: Multi-chain protocols, institutional platforms needing cross-chain asset and message transfer
companies.key_differentiator: Cross-chain messaging protocol enabling assets and data to move between blockchains securely — as institutional stacks become multi-chain the ability to move value across chains without centralized exchange intermediaries becomes critical infrastructure
companies.strategic_move: Expanding institutional partnerships, deepening integration with tokenization platforms building multi-chain asset issuance

companies.regulatory_status: Decentralized protocol architecture
companies.client_profile: DeFi protocols, cross-chain applications, institutional platforms requiring omnichain connectivity
companies.key_differentiator: Omnichain interoperability protocol — alternative cross-chain messaging architecture to Axelar with different trust assumptions and expanding institutional adoption as multi-chain becomes the institutional standard
companies.strategic_move: Expanding institutional use cases, building toward becoming the standard cross-chain messaging layer for institutional digital asset infrastructure
